title: Umbilical Cord Blood-Derived Cell Therapy for Preterm Brain Injury Mechanisms in HIE

input.description: Umbilical cord blood-derived cells demonstrate regenerative potential in preterm brain injury through multiple mechanisms: immunomodulation, trophic factor secretion, and potential cell replacement. While HIE differs from prematurity-related injury, both involve white matter damage, neuroinflammation, and disrupted neurodevelopment. The graph references cord blood cells as an active area of neonatal regenerative medicine research. Given Aleksandra's diffuse injury pattern and the 0-2 year plasticity window, cord blood cell infusion could provide paracrine neuroprotection and support endogenous repair. Duke's established cord blood banking and cell therapy infrastructure makes this particularly feasible for the family.
